title: fix: remove default system prompts from general-purpose models
author: pollinations-ai[bot]

gist.headline: Letting models speak for themselves
gist.blurb: We've stopped whispering default scripts into the ears of our general-purpose text models. Now, when you chat with Claude, GPT, or Llama without a system prompt, you get their pure, native personalities.
gist.summary: Removed the auto-injected conversational system prompt from general-purpose text models to allow them to use their native behaviors.
gist.impact: Models will no longer suffer from outdated self-identification or forced personality traits when a system message isn't provided, giving you purer, more accurate responses.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee removing old sticky notes (scripts/prompts) from the screen of a cute monitor robot, allowing the robot to show its true happy face. Represents removing forced personalities and letting the native model shine.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot peeling off old sticky notes from the screen of a chunky monitor robot. The robot looks happy and relieved, screen glowing lime green (#ecf874). Soft pastel lavender and cream gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, chunky sprites, emotionally warm.
